rulings.Case Title: KAREN KOWALSKI et al VS. A.W. CHESTERTON COMPANY et al

rulings.Case Title: BETTY UTTERBACK VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION For Relief Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure §473(A)(1)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 2, 2008, line 2, DEFENDANT FOSTER WHEELER LLC'S MOTION For Relief Pursuant To Code Of Civil Procedure §473(A)(1) IS DENIED, ABSENT A STIPULATION TO SHORTEN THE 75 DAY NOTICE PERIOD (CCP 437c(a)) IN LIGHT OF CURRENT TRIAL DATE. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS KAILATH VS. YVONNE DE LOS REYES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn In Oppos. To X-Defts Application An Order Determining Good Faith Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 2, 2008, line 3, CROSS-COMPLAINANT JASBIR WALIA, NATIONAL FINANCIAL INVESTMENT CORP'S Motion In Opposition To Cross-Defendants Application An Order Determining Good Faith Settlement. THE MOTION IS DENIED. THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT AND MUTUAL RELEASE OF ALL CLAIMS HAS NOT BEEN EXECUTED. THE STIPULATION FOR SETTLEMENT EXECUTED ON AUGUST 23, 2006 IS CONTINGENT ON THE ISSUANCE OF PERMITS AND THAT HAS NOT OCCURRED. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: QUEEN ESTHER PIERCE et al VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Continue Trial Date
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 2, 2008, line 4, DEFENDANT CALAVERAS ASBESTOS, LTD.'S Motion To Continue Trial Date IS OFF CALENDAR, TO BE RE-NOTICED IN DEPARTMENT 206. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: JUDITH IVORY et al VS. TLSF, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Mandatory Relief From An Order Of The Court And, Alternatively, Discretionary Relief (Ccp 473); Appendix
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 2, 2008, line 5, PLAINTIFF PAUL LEBLANC'S Motion For Mandatory Relief From An Order Of The Court And, Alternatively, Discretionary Relief (Ccp 473). THE MOTION IS DENIED. THE WEIGHT OF AUTHORITY HOLDS THAT THE MANDATORY PROVISIONS OF CCP SECTION 473(b) ARE NOT APPLICABLE TO THIS MOTION. SEE, ENGLISH V. IKON BUSINESS SOLUTIONS, INC. (2001) 94 CAL.APP.4TH 130, 138; HOUSSAIN V. HOUSSAIN (2007) 157 CAL.APP.4TH 454. LEBLANC IS NOT ENTITLED TO DISCRETIONARY RELIEF UNDER CCP SECTION 473. NO DECLARATION HAS BEEN SUBMITTED FROM LEBLANC AND THE RECORD REFLECTS NOTICE OF THE OPT OUT DATE TO ALL CLASS MEMBERS. THE DECLARATIONS OF KLIMENKO AND SCHUEBER FAIL TO DEMONSTRATE EXCUSABLE NEGLECT BY ESTABLISHING THAT EACH ACTED AS WOULD A PRUDENT ATTORNEY SEE E.G. AMBROSE V. MICHELIN NORTH AMERICA, INC. (2005) 134 CAL.APP.4TH 1350, 1355. THE FEDERAL CASES RELIED ON BY LEBLANC'S COUNSEL INVOLVE FACTS MATERIALLY DIFFERENT THEN THOSE AT ISSUE. ALLOWING AN OPT OUT AT THIS TIME, RAISES POLICY ISSUES RELATED TO THE APPROVAL OF CLASS ACTIONS THAT LEBLANC HAS NOT ADDRESSED. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA TANJUTCO-SMITH et al VS. IRENE KHO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Amend Judgment To Add Judgment Debtor
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 2, 2008, line 6, PLAINTIFFS MARIA TANJUTCO-SMITH AND ROBERT GARABEDIAN'S Motion To Amend Judgment To Add Judgment Debtor. THE MOTION IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ESTABLISHED THAT FUNDS ARE COMMINGLED OR THAT CORPORATE FORMALITIES ARE NOT FOLLOWED OR THAT THE CORPORATION IS A MERE SHELL. BECAUSE NEITHER PARTY HAS SUBMITTED THE TRANSCRIPT OF THE DEBTOR'S EXAMINATION, THE COURT IS LEFT WITH THE DECLARATIONS OF THE PARTIES. BASED ON THIS RECORD, PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO MEET HER BURDEN OF PROOF. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: STEPHEN E LAWRENCE VS. SEAN MCKEAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Award Of Attornes Fees And Judgement Against Pltf Stephen E. Lawrence; Request For Judicial Notice
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 2, 2008, line 7, DEFENDANTS SEAN MCKEAN AND SHAWN MCILVENNA'S Motion For Award Of Attorneys Fees And Judgement Against Plaintiff Stephen E. Lawrence. THE MOTION IS DENIED BECAUSE PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT WAS NOT BASED ON THE LANDLORD TENANT LEASE AGREEMENT. PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS RELATED TO TORTIOUS CONDUCT, ACTS SEPARATE AND DISTINCT FROM THE LEASE AGREEMENT. BECAUSE FEE PROVISIONS ARE STRICTLY CONSTRUED, THE COURT DOES NOT READ THIS AGREEMENT TO EMBRACE PLAINTIFF'S CLAIMS. SEE, E.G. STOCKTON THEATRES V. PALERMO (1954) 124 CAL.APP.2D 353. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: DENNIS HILL et al VS. DAMERON HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 2, 2008, line 8. DEFENDANT DAMERON HOSPITAL ASSOCIATION'S AMENDED MOTION TO STRIKE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO DECEMBER 11, 2008 PER NOVEMBER 26, 2008 RULING. MOVING PARTY TO PROVIDE COURTESY COPY OF REPLY WITH A COVER LETTER REFLECTING THE NEW HEARING DATE. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: ANNE W THAYER VS. CENTRAL CALIFORNIA BANK et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 2, 2008, line 9, DEFENDANT CENTRAL CALIFORNIA BANK'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION. IT APPEARS THAT PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM IS BARRED BY THE 2 YEAR STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS CCP SECTION 339 UNLESS PLAINTIFF IS CAPABLE OF PLEADING THE EXISTENCE OF A WRITTEN AGREEMENT WITH DEFENDANT. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION. THE CRLA IS NOT APPLICABLE TO REAL PROPERTY TRANSACTIONS. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED ONLY A PRIVATE DISPUTE. THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED AS TO THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR PLAINTIFF HAS NOT ALLEGED A FALSE ADVERTISING CLAIM. IF PLAINTIFF SEEKS LEASE TO AMEND, PLAINTIFF MUST APPEAR AND STATE WHAT IT IS PLAINTIFF INTENDS TO ALLEGE. =(302/PJM/JW)

rulings.Case Title: FIA CARD SERVICES,N.A. FKA, MBNA AMERICA BANK NA VS. JAMES DEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motioon To Confirm Arb Award
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 2, 2008, line 10, PETITIONER FIA CARD SERVICES, N.A. FKA, MBNA AMERICA BANK NA'S Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award is granted. No opposition filed. =(302/PJM)

rulings.Case Title: KEITH SWEENEY et al VS. JAMES ARRINGTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Moiton To Quash Service Of Summons And Three Day Notice Of In The Alternative To Set Aside Default
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 2, 2008, line 11, DEFENDANT JAMES ARRINGTON'S Motion To Quash Service Of Summons And Three Day Notice Of In The Alternative To Set Aside Default IS DENIED, 5 DAYS TO PLEAD. PLAINTIFF HAS DEMONSTRATED REASONABLE EFFORTS TO SERVE DEFENDANT WITH THE COMPLAINT AND HAS EFFECTED SERVICE BY UTILIZING THE METHOD AUTHORIZING SUBSTITUTED SERVICE OF THE COMPLAINT. A MOTION TO QUASH IS NOT A VEHICLE TO CHALLENGE SERVICE OF THE 3 DAY NOTICE TO QUIT, RATHER, THIS IS A DEFENSE TO PLAINTIFF'S CLAIM. =(302/PJM/PB)
